meta.description: Systematic verification of 20 unconfirmed/alleged claims from the Argentine monopoly investigation
meta.methodology: Web search verification against news sources, government filings, court records, and financial reports

claims.claim: Telecom-Cablevision merger conditions were never fully met
claims.findings: The CNDC imposed strict conditions in 2018: divestiture of broadband assets in 28 localities (143,464 clients), spectrum return of up to 80 MHz, wholesale internet service obligations, and cuadruple-play delays. Multiple sources confirm these conditions were set, but compliance monitoring has been weak. The CNDC ordered asset sales in fixed broadband but enforcement appears incomplete, especially given that the Telefonica acquisition in 2025 further concentrated the market to ~70% of telecom services under one group.

sources.title: CNDC ordena venta de activos en banda ancha fija e impone obligaciones a Telecom
Aprobaron la fusion de Telecom-Cablevision y le imponen condiciones - Perfil
Aprueban con limitaciones la fusion de Cablevision y Telecom - Ambito

claims.claim: Pending 2025 Telefonica acquisition at USD 1.245B, CNDC objected in 127-page report
claims.findings: Confirmed. On Feb 24, 2025, Telefonica Argentina was sold to Telecom (Clarin Group 40%, Fintech/David Martinez 40%, minority shareholders 20%) for USD 1.245 billion. The CNDC objected in June 2025, identifying significant competition risks — the acquisition would leave ~70% of telecom services under one economic group. In March 2025, the Secretary of Industry and Commerce imposed preventive measures prohibiting integration of teams or exchange of competitively sensitive information. Telecom was told to wait 6 months. The deal remains under regulatory scrutiny as of March 2026.

sources.title: Telefonica de Argentina se vendio a Telecom por US$1245 millones - LA NACION
Telecom Argentina debera esperar seis meses - Infobae
Telecom consiguio u$s 200 millones para avanzar en la cuestionada compra - El Cronista

claims.correction: The claim says 'pending' but the sale agreement was signed Feb 2025. CNDC objection confirmed. The '127-page report' detail could not be independently verified but the substance of the objection is confirmed.

claims.claim: ENACOM interventor Ozores is former Telecom employee
claims.findings: Confirmed with correction. Juan Martin Ozores was designated ENACOM interventor by Decree 89/2024 on Jan 29, 2024. He was a consultant for Telecom between 2013-2016, previously worked for Movicom BellSouth (1996-2005), was Director of Worldmedia Consulting, and worked for Strata Analytics Group (Florida). He has 25+ years in telecom industry. The claim says 'former Telecom employee' — technically he was a consultant to Telecom, not a direct employee, but the revolving-door concern is substantiated.

sources.title: Quien es Juan Martin Ozores, el hombre que Milei designo Interventor del ENACOM
El Enacom fue intervenido por 180 dias: Un ex empleado de Movistar es el interventor

claims.correction: Ozores was a Telecom consultant (2013-2016), not a direct employee. He was also a former Movicom BellSouth employee (1996-2005). Some outlets describe him as 'ex-empleado de Movistar' (Telefonica subsidiary).

claims.claim: Edenor + Edesur combined ARS 274B profit in 9 months (44% above inflation)
claims.findings: Multiple sources confirm Edenor and Edesur combined earned close to ARS 300 billion in the first 9 months of 2024. La Izquierda Diario specifically states 'casi $300.000 millones en los primeros 9 meses del ano'. Edenor's full-year 2024 profit was ARS 272.128 billion (42% increase YoY). Edesur profits were significant but declining in late 2024. The '274B in 9 months' figure is in the right ballpark. Edenor's full-year gains increased 42%, consistent with the '44% above inflation' claim given 2024 inflation of ~117%.

sources.title: Tarifazos: entre Edenor y Edesur ganaron casi $300.000 millones en los primeros 9 meses
Por la suba de tarifas, Edenor tuvo mayores ingresos en 2024 y sus ganancias aumentaron 42% - Ambito
Entre Edenor y Edesur ganaron $160.000 millones en el primer semestre del ano

claims.correction: The combined 9-month figure is closer to ARS 300B rather than 274B. The 274B may refer to Edenor alone for full-year 2024. The '44% above inflation' framing needs context: profits grew 42% YoY in nominal terms, but inflation was 117.8% in 2024, so real profits actually declined. However, tariff increases were the driver.

claims.claim: Gas transport duopoly TGS/TGN licenses extended to 2047
claims.findings: Confirmed. The government extended TGS's license via Decree 495/2025 (published in Official Gazette July 2025) for 20 additional years starting Dec 28, 2027, extending to Dec 28, 2047. TGN also requested the same extension. The original 35-year licenses (from 1992 privatization of Gas del Estado) were set to expire Dec 28, 2027. The Ley Bases enabled a 20-year extension (previously only 10 years to 2037 was possible). A public hearing was held Oct 21, 2024, and the agreement was signed July 11, 2025.

sources.title: TGN y TGS le pidieron al Estado que extienda hasta 2047 sus licencias - EconoJournal
Renuevan hasta 2047 licencia a TGS para transportar gas del sur - Ambito
El Gobierno extiende por 20 anos una licencia clave de operacion en el transporte de gas

claims.claim: Carlos Casares (24 years at Techint) now regulates gas sector
claims.findings: Confirmed with corrections. Carlos Alberto Maria Casares was appointed ENARGAS interventor in January 2024. He previously worked at Tecpetrol (Techint Group's petroleum company) for 'more than 16 years' (not 24 as claimed), where he was in charge of natural gas sales. He resigned in January 2026 after being excluded from the new unified energy regulator (ENRGE). The revolving-door concern is substantiated — he directly regulated the sector he formerly worked in.

sources.title: Ex gerente de Techint fue designado interventor del Enargas - Mendoza Post
Enargas: Carlos Casares presento su renuncia como interventor - EconoJournal
Carlos Casares renuncio al ENARGAS - elDiarioAR

claims.correction: Casares worked at Tecpetrol (Techint Group) for 16+ years, not 24 as claimed. He resigned from ENARGAS in January 2026.

claims.claim: CAME farm-to-shelf markup is 3.1-3.6x throughout 2024
claims.findings: The CAME IPOD index measures the origin-to-destination price gap for agricultural products. In January 2024, the markup was 3.8x. The claim of 3.1-3.6x throughout 2024 appears to be a conservative range — the actual data shows fluctuation but often exceeded 3.6x. The IPOD confirms substantial farm-to-shelf markups consistently above 3x throughout 2024.

sources.title: IPOD enero: el ano comenzo con una brecha de precios campo-gondola de 3,8 veces - CAME
Indicador de Precios en Origen y Destino / IPOD - CAME

claims.correction: The 3.1-3.6x range appears to understate the actual IPOD values. January 2024 showed 3.8x. The range is directionally correct but may be conservative.

claims.claim: Ledesma controls 85% of sugar with 2 allies
claims.findings: Ledesma (Blaquier family) produces approximately 75% of white sugar in Argentina. Eight companies control 90% of total sugar refining. Four groups maintain control of six ingenios, concentrating ~50% of production. The 85% figure with 2 allies could not be precisely confirmed, but the oligopolistic structure is well-documented. The concentration is very high but the exact 85% figure with specifically 2 allies requires better sourcing.

sources.title: Los duenos del azucar: las ocho empresas que concentran el negocio - La Izquierda Diario
Veinte empresas deciden que comen y a que precio 45 millones de personas
Quien es Ledesma - El gigante agroindustrial - Bichos de Campo

claims.correction: Ledesma controls ~75% of white sugar, not 85%. Eight companies control 90% of total refining. The '2 allies' formulation is not precisely confirmed.

claims.claim: Mastellone controls 90% fluid milk in Buenos Aires
claims.findings: Mastellone (La Serenisima) is dominant in the dairy market. The company 'concentrates 90% of fluid milk sales in the internal market' according to some sources, but Nielsen data shows ~58% national market share in fluid milk. Brand penetration is 89% (9 out of 10 households). More than 90% of production goes to domestic market. The 90% figure likely refers to Buenos Aires metro area specifically or to brand recognition/household penetration rather than strict volume share.

sources.title: Quien es Mastellone Hnos - Bichos de Campo
Arcor quiere quedarse con Mastellone - El Cronista

claims.correction: National fluid milk market share is ~58% per Nielsen. The 90% figure may refer to Buenos Aires metro area specifically or to household penetration (89%). Mastellone is dominant but the 90% claim needs geographic qualification.

claims.claim: Cuadernos case: 86 defendants, trial ongoing since Nov 2025
claims.findings: Confirmed. The trial began November 6, 2025 at Federal Oral Court No. 7. The total number of defendants is 87 (not 86): 19-22 ex-officials and 65 businessmen. This is described as the largest corruption trial in Argentine judicial history, with 440+ witnesses cited. As of March 2026, the trial continues with preliminary defense motions after the prosecution completed its accusation reading in December 2025.

sources.title: Comenzó el juicio por los Cuadernos de las coimas - Infobae
Cuadernos de la Corrupcion: CFK fue acusada de ser la jefa de 86 personas - El Cronista
Causa Cuadernos: la hoja de ruta de un juicio oral historico - Perfil

claims.correction: Total defendants are 87, not 86. Trial started Nov 6, 2025 (confirmed).

claims.claim: Odebrecht paid USD 35M in bribes in Argentina, zero convictions
claims.findings: Confirmed. Odebrecht confessed to paying at least USD 35 million in bribes in Argentina between 2007-2014. As of March 2026, there are zero convictions. The case has been described as 'stalled in the justice system' (Cadena 3). Key developments: Supreme Court upheld updated embargo (April 2025); trial began May 2025 but with disruptions over a Milei decree; Casacion Penal rejected prescription claims in March 2026. Argentina is described as being 'on the podium of impunity' regarding Odebrecht.

sources.title: Sin condenas ni multas: la Argentina esta en el podio de la impunidad - LA NACION
Casacion descarto la prescripcion de la causa Odebrecht - Infobae
El juicio por el caso Odebrecht empezo con sobresaltos - LA NACION

claims.claim: Zero Argentine-owned lithium producers
claims.findings: Substantially confirmed. All six active lithium operations in Argentina are controlled by foreign capital: Rio Tinto (Australian/British, acquired Arcadium Lithium for USD 6.7B), Ganfeng Lithium (Chinese, operates Cauchari-Olaroz and Mariana), and other international miners. 'Lithium Argentina' is a publicly traded company (formerly Lithium Americas Argentina) listed on TSX/NYSE — despite the name, it is Canadian-headquartered. No purely Argentine-owned company is currently producing lithium at scale. Provincial governments hold royalty interests but not production operations.

sources.title: Litio en Argentina: recursos, desarrollos, y que empresas lo explotan - AgendAR
La Argentina acelera su apuesta por el litio - LA NACION
Las 41 empresas que apuestan al litio en Argentina - Bloomberg Linea

claims.claim: Veladero cyanide spills had no trial after 7+ years
claims.findings: Confirmed. The original Veladero cyanide spill occurred September 2015 (1,072,000 liters of cyanide solution). As of March 2026, over 10 years later, no oral trial has been held. The case is pending before Judge Servini de Cubria; hearings scheduled for 2020 were suspended for COVID and never rescheduled. The prosecution appealed to Casacion Penal. A new cyanide spill was detected in November 2025. The case exemplifies judicial impunity in Argentine mining.

sources.title: Barrick Gold lo hizo: diez anos del mayor derrame minero de la historia argentina - Tierra Viva
El modelo minero de San Juan: Barrick Gold, el mayor derrame de la historia y la impunidad - Tierra Viva
Barrick Gold va a juicio por el mayor desastre ambiental - Foro Ambiental

claims.correction: The claim says '7+ years' but it has now been over 10 years since the original spill (September 2015).

claims.claim: PAMI markups up to 1,327% on oncology drugs (anastrozol)
claims.findings: Confirmed. A criminal complaint filed by Civic Coalition leaders Elisa Carrio and Hernan Reyes documented that PAMI purchased anastrozol from GP Pharm at $13,192/unit via Framework Agreement while the same medication cost $924/unit via public bidding — a 1,327% markup. During 2023, PAMI overpaid $273 million on essential oncology medications. Another example: bevacizumab was paid $91,121/unit vs $17,000 at public auction. The complaint targets ACE Oncology and at least five pharmaceutical laboratories for anticompetitive practices.

sources.title: Denuncian al PAMI y a laboratorios por sobreprecios en la compra de remedios oncologicos - Infobae
Denuncian que el PAMI pago medicamentos oncologicos hasta 16 veces mas - LA NACION
Escandalo en Pami: denuncian sobreprecios del 1400% - MercedesYA

claims.claim: Drug prices 26% above Latin American average
claims.findings: Confirmed directionally. Sources indicate that a hypothetical basket of medications in Argentina was 26% more expensive than the Latin American average. Additional context: Infobae reported in September 2024 that Argentine drug prices are more expensive than in Spain and the United States for some medications, with price variations exceeding 1,000%. Argentines need to work 4 additional hours compared to the regional average to cover basic medicine costs. However, in 2024 overall drug prices rose 90.9% vs 117.8% inflation, showing some real-terms decline.

sources.title: Los medicamentos en Argentina son mas caros que en Espana y Estados Unidos - Infobae
Informe de precios de medicamentos - CESA

claims.claim: BCRA head Bausili prosecuted for conflict of interest
claims.findings: Confirmed. Santiago Bausili, president of BCRA, has been prosecuted (procesado) for 'negociaciones incompatibles con la funcion publica' (conflict of interest). Judge Casanello processed him twice. The Sala II of Camara Federal de Casacion Penal rejected his nullity appeal in December 2024. The case stems from his time as Secretary of Finance under Macri, when he continued receiving ~USD 200,000 in stock and bonuses from Deutsche Bank while leading government debt placement operations that benefited the same bank. The state's debt grew to USD 100 billion during this period.

sources.title: Casacion confirmo el procesamiento del presidente del Banco Central - El Economista
Nuevo reves judicial para Santiago Bausili: ratificaron que existio conflicto de intereses - Ambito
Bausili, investigado por corrupcion - Pagina12

claims.claim: Prisma Medios de Pago controls 80% credit cards
claims.findings: Confirmed. Prisma is the largest payment processor in Argentina, handling more than 80% of credit and debit card processing activities. Visa completed its acquisition of Prisma Medios de Pago S.A.U. and Newpay S.A.U. on February 27, 2026. Prisma processes over 6 billion transactions annually. The acquirer business (Payway) was excluded from the Visa deal and remains with Advent. This concentration is now under Visa's direct ownership.

sources.title: Visa completa la compra de Prisma: los detalles del deal del ano - El Cronista
Visa compro Prisma y expande su negocio de pagos digitales en la Argentina - Infobae

claims.correction: The claim is about 'credit cards' but Prisma handles both credit and debit card processing. The 80% figure covers total card processing, not just credit. Also, Visa now owns Prisma as of Feb 2026.

claims.claim: Aluar pays USD 16-26/MWh vs market $70-80/MWh
claims.findings: Confirmed. Aluar's contract with Hidroelectrica Futaleufu provides electricity at approximately USD 20.78/MWh (at aluminum price of USD 2,200/t), with a floor of USD 9.12/MWh. Recent reports indicate charges over USD 26/MWh. Meanwhile, market wholesale electricity prices range USD 70-80/MWh. Aluar owns 60.20% of Hidroelectrica Futaleufu, with Chubut province holding 33.51%. The dam allocates 95% of its energy exclusively to Aluar. The concession expires June 2026.

sources.title: El gobierno nacional garantiza el precio de la Energia a ALUAR - Argentina.gob.ar
Hidroelectrica Futaleufu vende energia subsidiada a Aluar - Red43
Hidroelectrica Futaleufu - HCDN proyecto de ley

claims.claim: FATE tire factory permanently closed Feb 2026
claims.findings: Confirmed. FATE announced its permanent closure on February 18, 2026, laying off all 920 employees at its Virreyes plant (San Fernando, Buenos Aires). The plant occupied 40 hectares with 157,000+ m2 covered area and capacity for 5+ million tires per year. The closure was triggered by tariff reduction on imported truck/bus tires from 35% to 16%, combined with export duties and cost differentials the company called 'insalvable' (unbridgeable). This ended 80+ years of Argentine tire manufacturing.

sources.title: Fate anuncio su cierre definitivo - Infobae
Cerro la fabrica de neumaticos FATE y despidio mas de 900 trabajadores - Ambito
Cronologia de una caida: los tres momentos que derivaron en el cierre definitivo de Fate - Infobae

claims.claim: MercadoLibre 62% marketplace GMV, 80% retail e-commerce
claims.findings: The 62% marketplace GMV figure is confirmed across multiple analyst reports and financial sources. MercadoLibre commands roughly 62% of marketplace GMV in Argentina, with Amazon, Fravega and Carrefour following at a distance. Q1 2025 showed FX-neutral GMV surging 126% YoY in Argentina. However, the '80% retail e-commerce' figure could not be independently verified with a specific source. The company's dominance is clear but the 80% figure may conflate marketplace GMV with total retail e-commerce, which also includes direct-to-consumer channels.

sources.title: MercadoLibre: Dominant in LatAm E-Commerce and Fintech
Argentina E-commerce Market Size, Growth, Forecast - Mordor Intelligence
Mercado Libre Caps Stellar 2025 Performance - BusinessWire

claims.correction: 62% marketplace GMV confirmed. The 80% retail e-commerce claim could not be verified with a specific source.

key_corrections: TELECOM-03: Ozores was a Telecom consultant (2013-2016), not a direct employee. Also ex-Movicom BellSouth.
ENERGY-03: Casares worked 16+ years at Tecpetrol/Techint, not 24. He resigned ENARGAS Jan 2026.
ENERGY-01: Combined 9-month profit closer to ARS 300B, not 274B. The 274B may be Edenor full-year.
FOOD-01: CAME IPOD was 3.8x in Jan 2024, often exceeding the claimed 3.1-3.6x range.
FOOD-02: Ledesma controls ~75% of white sugar, not 85%. Eight firms control 90% total.
FOOD-03: National fluid milk share is ~58% per Nielsen. The 90% may be Buenos Aires metro or household penetration.
MINING-02: Now 10+ years since the spill, not 7+.
BANKING-02: Visa completed acquisition of Prisma in Feb 2026, changing ownership structure.
